attacks_attempted.payload: empty hotelName, checkout before checkin, oversized optional strings
<script>alert(1)</script> in hotel/notes fields
call create/update/cancel with no write permission
create accommodation in event A using registrationId from another event/person
call create action directly for a person with no active travel record
create/update/cancel accommodation and inspect side effects for audit write
create/update/cancel accommodation and inspect side effects for cascade emission
concurrent update/cancel after stale read with unchanged row id but changed state
large rooming metadata strings within and above schema limits

bugs_found.description: createAccommodationRecord accepted arbitrary registration IDs, allowing an accommodation row in one event to be linked to a registration from a different event or person.
bugs_found.fix: Validated registrationId with eventId + personId scoping before insert.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/accommodation.test.ts > rejects registration IDs from another event/person

bugs_found.description: The travel-first business rule only existed in the form picker, so direct server-action calls could create accommodation rows for people without any active travel record.
bugs_found.fix: Added a server-side active-travel check before accommodation creation.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/accommodation.test.ts > rejects creating accommodation without a non-cancelled travel record

bugs_found.description: Accommodation create, update, and cancel mutations completed without writing the required audit log entry or dispatching their cascade event, bypassing downstream notifications and flagging.
bugs_found.fix: Wired create/update/cancel through writeAudit and emitCascadeEvent with accommodation-specific payloads; update emits only when trigger fields change.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/accommodation.test.ts > writes an audit log and emits a cascade event on create

bugs_found.description: updateAccommodationRecord and cancelAccommodationRecord fetched the row under event scope but wrote back by id only, so a concurrent status change could still be overwritten after the read.
bugs_found.fix: Added compare-and-swap style update filters using eventId, non-cancelled status, and updatedAt when available; stale writes now fail loudly.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/accommodation.test.ts > blocks concurrent updates that changed the record after it was read

attacks_attempted.payload: route eventId=not-a-uuid with otherwise valid attendance query input
route eventId for event A with body eventId for event B
attendance row scoped to event A joined to registration metadata by registrationId without event-scoping the joined registration table
concurrent check-in between total, by-method, and by-session aggregate SELECTs under default Postgres READ COMMITTED transaction isolation
date/sessionId query payloads containing SQL metacharacters
read attendance as read-only user and attempt write escalation through this read-only module
oversized/unknown query fields and unbounded attendance list request
<script>alert(1)</script> in returned person/registration fields

bugs_found.description: Attendance read actions validated the body eventId after assertEventAccess and did not validate getConfirmedRegistrationCount eventId at all, so malformed route IDs could reach auth/database queries before Zod rejected them.
bugs_found.fix: Added route eventId validation before auth/database access for all attendance read actions.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/attendance.test.ts > rejects a malformed route eventId before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: listAttendanceRecords joined event_registrations by registrationId only. A bad or legacy attendance row in the active event could surface registrationNumber/category metadata from another event because the joined table was not scoped to eventId.
bugs_found.fix: Scoped the registration left join with eventRegistrations.eventId = active eventId and rejected body/route eventId mismatches.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/attendance.test.ts > event-scopes joined registration metadata to prevent cross-tenant leakage

bugs_found.description: getAttendanceStats performed three aggregate SELECTs in a default transaction. Under Postgres READ COMMITTED, concurrent check-ins can appear between SELECTs and return a total that disagrees with by-method/by-session counts.
bugs_found.fix: Requested repeatable-read isolation for the stats transaction so all aggregates use one stable snapshot.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/attendance.test.ts > uses repeatable-read isolation so concurrent check-ins cannot tear aggregate snapshots

attacks_attempted.payload: route eventId=not-a-uuid with otherwise valid offline batch body
route eventId for event A with body eventId for event B
offline record with sessionId from another event while QR token belongs to the active event
QR payload eventId from another event
call processBatchSync without write permission
double-submit the same offline scan concurrently
oversized batch, QR payload, and deviceId values
SQL metacharacters inside QR payload and deviceId
<script>alert(1)</script> in QR payload and deviceId
force registration/person/insert database errors

bugs_found.description: processBatchSync called assertEventAccess before validating the route eventId, so malformed route IDs reached auth work and then returned an item-level invalid result instead of rejecting the request.
bugs_found.fix: Validate the route eventId with the offline sync eventId schema before auth or database work.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/batch-sync.test.ts > rejects a malformed route eventId before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: processBatchSync parsed input.eventId but never compared it to the route eventId, allowing stale or cross-event bodies to be accepted under a different route scope.
bugs_found.fix: Reject body/route eventId mismatches before auth and use the validated scoped eventId for all QR comparisons, registration lookup, deterministic IDs, and inserts.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/batch-sync.test.ts > rejects body and route eventId mismatches before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: Offline sync accepted any UUID-shaped sessionId and inserted it into an attendance row for the active event without verifying that the session belonged to that event.
bugs_found.fix: Validate non-null sessionId values with an event-scoped sessions lookup before registration lookup or attendance insert.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/batch-sync.test.ts > rejects a sessionId that is not scoped to the active event

attacks_attempted.payload: call updateEventBranding with logoStorageKey/headerImageStorageKey pointing at branding/{OTHER_EVENT_ID}/... then call getBrandingImageUrls
call uploadBrandingImage with runtime imageType '../../header'
upload image/svg+xml containing <script> and onload
upload file declared image/png whose bytes are plain text
read event.branding, concurrently update the same event, then save stale merged JSON
call update/upload/delete branding with read-only access
upload files over 5MB and submit max-length branding text fields
SQL metacharacters in branding text fields and storage key strings
__proto__ and constructor.prototype fields in updateEventBranding input
invalid eventId, invalid colors, wrong MIME type, empty file, malformed storage keys
oversized sender/footer/prefix strings and control/unicode payloads
missing event, invalid MIME, stale update, and missing file error paths

bugs_found.description: updateEventBranding accepted arbitrary logo/header storage keys, and getBrandingImageUrls signed whatever was stored. A write-capable user could point one event at another event's R2 branding object and receive a signed URL for it.
bugs_found.fix: Validate direct storage-key updates against branding/{eventId}/{logo|header}/, sanitize legacy stored branding before returning it, and refuse to sign out-of-scope keys.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/branding.test.ts > rejects direct cross-event logo storage keys before updating; does not sign stored image keys outside the active event scope

bugs_found.description: uploadBrandingImage trusted the TypeScript-only imageType union at runtime. A direct caller could pass '../../header', producing a logical storage key outside the intended logo/header prefix.
bugs_found.fix: Validate imageType at runtime before auth, storage, or database work.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/branding.test.ts > rejects runtime-invalid image types before auth or storage work

bugs_found.description: Branding upload trusted file.type and allowed active SVG or spoofed image content to be stored and later rendered/signed as event branding.
bugs_found.fix: Validate PNG/JPEG/WebP magic bytes and reject active SVG constructs including script tags, event-handler attributes, javascript: URLs, foreignObject, iframe, object, and embed.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/branding.test.ts > rejects spoofed PNG uploads whose bytes are not PNG data; rejects active SVG payloads before upload

bugs_found.description: updateEventBranding read the current branding JSON, merged a partial update, then wrote the full JSON object by event id only. Concurrent updates could silently clobber each other.
bugs_found.fix: Select events.updatedAt and use a compare-and-swap update filter with returning(); stale writes now fail before cache revalidation.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/branding.test.ts > blocks stale branding saves that race with another update

attacks_attempted.payload: route eventId=not-a-uuid and body eventId mismatch against the route scope
issued certificate row whose storageKey points at another event/type prefix
call bulkZipDownload without a certificate write role
distributed lock renew() returns false after 10 files, simulating TTL expiry or ownership loss mid-generation
second request while the event/type lock is already held
501 issued certificates in one ZIP request
metadata says tiny PDFs but downloaded bytes exceed the 200MB aggregate ceiling
certificate storageKey containing another event prefix or path traversal markers
invalid storage object / Redis failure paths

bugs_found.description: bulkZipDownload renewed its distributed lock every 10 files but swallowed both renew() failures and false returns. Once the lock expired or ownership moved to another process, the original request kept generating the ZIP, allowing concurrent duplicate bulk jobs for the same event/type.
bugs_found.fix: Fail closed on lock-renew loss. bulkZipDownload now aborts the job when renew() fails or returns false instead of continuing without exclusivity.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-bulk-zip.test.ts > aborts when the distributed lock can no longer be renewed mid-generation

attacks_attempted.payload: server-action eventId=not-a-uuid for recipient preview, bulk generation, and notification queueing
isCertificateGenerationEnabled throws Redis/provider error while the flag should gate generation
bulkGenerateCertificates recipientType=custom with personIds from another event
bulkGenerateCertificates recipientType=custom with empty or repeated personIds to queue a silent no-op/background job
sendCertificateNotifications with certificateIds from another event
sendCertificateNotifications with superseded/revoked certificateIds after certificate chain status changed
call bulk generation / notification actions without certificate write role
templateId, personIds, and certificateIds containing SQL metacharacters

bugs_found.description: The certificate-generation actions trusted the route/server-action eventId and called auth/database helpers before validating UUID shape, so malformed event scopes reached auth/database work instead of failing fast.
bugs_found.fix: Added explicit eventId validation at the start of recipient preview, bulk generation, and notification queue actions.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-generation.test.ts > rejects a malformed eventId before auth or database access; rejects a malformed eventId before auth, flags, or database access

bugs_found.description: bulkGenerateCertificates wrapped isCertificateGenerationEnabled() in a catch that only rethrew the 'currently disabled' branch, so any Redis/provider failure silently failed open and allowed certificate generation to continue while the feature gate was unavailable.
bugs_found.fix: Made the feature-flag gate fail closed: provider errors now abort generation with a generic availability-verification error instead of proceeding.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-generation.test.ts > fails closed when certificate generation availability cannot be verified

bugs_found.description: bulkGenerateCertificates accepted arbitrary custom personIds and queued Inngest work even when some IDs were not attached to the active event, letting direct callers smuggle cross-event recipients into the job payload and queue silent no-op jobs.
bugs_found.fix: Required custom recipient lists to be non-empty, bounded, deduplicated, and fully event-scoped before dispatching the bulk generation event.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-generation.test.ts > rejects custom personIds that are not attached to the active event

bugs_found.description: sendCertificateNotifications queued async work for any UUID-shaped certificate IDs, even if the IDs belonged to another event or had already been superseded/revoked, so direct callers could queue misleading or stale notification jobs.
bugs_found.fix: Validated that every certificate ID belongs to the active event and is still in issued status before dispatching the notification job.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-generation.test.ts > rejects certificateIds from another event before queuing notifications; rejects superseded or revoked certificates before queuing notifications

attacks_attempted.payload: server-action eventId=not-a-uuid across issue/revoke/list/get/download flows
issue a delegate certificate while pointing at an active CME template
issue a certificate with eligibilityBasisId from another event/person, or manual eligibility with an arbitrary record id
regenerate a certificate while the current issued row changes before supersession
revoke a certificate after validation while another request supersedes it
double-click manual resend; idempotencyKey includes Date.now so every click bypasses Redis dedupe
call write actions without certificate write role
SQL metacharacters in eventId, certificateId, templateId, personId, and verification token
<script>alert(1)</script> inside renderedVariablesJson and revokeReason
oversized/invalid ID arrays not accepted by this single-certificate action surface; large renderedVariablesJson remains bounded by request/runtime limits, not action-specific recursion

bugs_found.description: Certificate issuance actions called assertEventAccess before validating route/server-action eventId shape, so malformed event scopes reached auth/database-adjacent work instead of failing fast.
bugs_found.fix: Added local eventId validation and used the scoped eventId consistently in issue, revoke, list, get, download, and resend paths.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-issuance.test.ts > rejects a malformed eventId before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: issueCertificate accepted any active template id in the event without binding it to the requested certificateType, allowing direct callers to create certificate rows whose type did not match the immutable template snapshot.
bugs_found.fix: Scoped the active template lookup by certificateType and added a runtime certificateType equality guard before issuing.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-issuance.test.ts > rejects active templates whose certificate type does not match the requested certificate type

bugs_found.description: issueCertificate stored arbitrary eligibilityBasisId values without checking the referenced registration, attendance, assignment, or event role belonged to the active event and recipient person.
bugs_found.fix: Added event/person-scoped eligibility-basis validation for registration, attendance, session_assignment, and event_role references; manual eligibility now rejects backing record IDs.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-issuance.test.ts > rejects eligibility basis records that are not scoped to the event and person; rejects manual eligibility with an arbitrary backing record ID

bugs_found.description: Supersession inserted the new issued certificate before marking the old current row as superseded, which conflicts with the partial one-current unique index and allowed stale old-row updates by id only.
bugs_found.fix: Generated the new certificate id first, marked the old current row as superseded with eventId + status='issued' compare-and-swap semantics, then inserted the replacement certificate.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-issuance.test.ts > rejects stale supersession when the current certificate changed before it could be superseded

bugs_found.description: revokeCertificate validated that a row was issued, then updated by event/id only; a concurrent supersession could be overwritten into revoked after the validation read.
bugs_found.fix: Added status='issued' to the revoke update predicate and fail loudly when no row is updated.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-issuance.test.ts > rejects stale revocation when the certificate changed after validation

bugs_found.description: resendCertificateNotification used Date.now() in idempotencyKey, so rapid duplicate manual sends for the same certificate/channel were always treated as unique sends and bypassed Redis duplicate suppression.
bugs_found.fix: Changed manual certificate resend keys to the stable cert-send-{certificateId}-{channel} shape used by bulk certificate notifications.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate-issuance.test.ts > uses a stable idempotencyKey so rapid duplicate manual resends collapse

attacks_attempted.payload: route eventId=not-a-uuid on list/create/activate template actions
partial update with requiredVariablesJson not included in allowedVariablesJson
stale update/activate/archive after another admin changed template status or updatedAt
template lookup/update by id from another event
certificate template writes with non-certificate role
recipient search query containing wildcard and SQL metacharacters
<script>alert(1)</script> in template metadata fields
oversized search query and recipient limit escalation

bugs_found.description: Certificate template actions called assertEventAccess before validating the route eventId, so malformed route IDs reached auth work before being rejected.
bugs_found.fix: Added a shared route eventId validator and used the validated event scope for every certificate template read/write action before auth or database access.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate.test.ts > rejects a malformed route eventId before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: updateCertificateTemplate accepted partial updates where requiredVariablesJson was no longer a subset of allowedVariablesJson, allowing direct callers to persist impossible template configurations that createCertificateTemplate rejects.
bugs_found.fix: Validated the merged allowed/required variable sets during template updates and fail closed when a partial update would make the template invalid.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate.test.ts > rejects required variables that are not included in allowed variables

bugs_found.description: Template update, activate, and archive fetched the current row and then wrote back by id/event only, so stale concurrent requests could overwrite newer changes or revive a template after its status changed.
bugs_found.fix: Added compare-and-swap update predicates using eventId, current status, and updatedAt so stale writes and stale status transitions now fail with a refresh-required error.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/certificate.test.ts > rejects stale concurrent updates; rejects stale concurrent activations; rejects stale concurrent archives

attacks_attempted.payload: route eventId=not-a-uuid with otherwise valid search input
route eventId for event A with body eventId for event B
sessionId=not-a-uuid passed directly to the server action
sessionId from another event or a deleted session used to shape alreadyCheckedIn results
call searchRegistrationsForCheckIn without write permission
query with LIKE wildcards, backslashes, and SQL metacharacters
oversized and empty search queries
<script>alert(1)</script> in returned person fields

bugs_found.description: searchRegistrationsForCheckIn called assertEventAccess before validating the route eventId, so malformed route IDs reached auth work before Zod rejected them.
bugs_found.fix: Validate the route eventId up front and reuse the validated event scope for auth and every database query.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/checkin-search.test.ts > rejects a malformed route eventId before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: The action parsed input.eventId but never compared it to the route eventId, allowing stale or cross-event body payloads to be accepted under a different route scope.
bugs_found.fix: Reject body/route eventId mismatches before auth or database access.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/checkin-search.test.ts > rejects body and route eventId mismatches before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: The optional sessionId argument was never validated or event-scoped, so direct callers could pass malformed, deleted, or foreign-session IDs to shape alreadyCheckedIn state without proving the session belonged to the active event.
bugs_found.fix: Validate sessionId shape before auth and require any non-null sessionId to resolve to a session in the active event before searching or checking attendance.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/checkin-search.test.ts > rejects a malformed sessionId before auth or database access; rejects a sessionId that is not scoped to the active event

attacks_attempted.payload: route eventId=not-a-uuid with otherwise valid QR/manual check-in bodies
route eventId for event A with body eventId for event B
optional sessionId from another event passed to QR/manual check-in
call processQrScan/processManualCheckIn without write permission
double-submit the same QR/manual check-in concurrently
oversized qrPayload and deviceId strings
SQL metacharacters inside qrPayload, registrationId, and deviceId
<script>alert(1)</script> in qrPayload and deviceId
force missing-registration and duplicate-insert paths

bugs_found.description: processQrScan and processManualCheckIn called assertEventAccess before validating the route eventId, so malformed route IDs reached auth work before Zod rejected them.
bugs_found.fix: Validate the route eventId up front and reuse the validated event scope for auth, QR validation, duplicate detection, inserts, and revalidation.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/checkin.test.ts > rejects a malformed route eventId before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: The check-in actions parsed input.eventId but never compared it to the route eventId, allowing stale or cross-event body payloads to be accepted under a different route scope.
bugs_found.fix: Reject body/route eventId mismatches before auth or database access.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/checkin.test.ts > rejects route/body eventId mismatches before auth or database access

bugs_found.description: The optional sessionId was never validated against the active event in QR or manual check-in, so direct callers could shape duplicate detection and insert attendance rows against a foreign event's session.
bugs_found.fix: Require any non-null sessionId to resolve to a session in the active event before duplicate detection or inserts.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/checkin.test.ts > rejects sessionIds that do not belong to the active event before registration lookup

attacks_attempted.payload: route eventId=not-a-uuid for dashboard metrics and attention reads
query dashboard actions with another event's UUID
call dashboard reads as read-only user
SQL metacharacters inside eventId
<script>alert(1)</script> through eventId and dashboard labels
oversized eventId strings and repeated dashboard refreshes
concurrent writes between dashboard aggregate SELECTs under default READ COMMITTED isolation
click needs-attention links generated by the dashboard action
force auth failures and invalid event IDs

bugs_found.description: getDashboardMetrics and getNeedsAttention each executed multiple aggregate queries inside a default transaction. Under Postgres READ COMMITTED, concurrent registration/notification/flag writes can land between those SELECTs and return torn dashboard snapshots.
bugs_found.fix: Requested repeatable-read isolation for both dashboard transactions so each dashboard read uses a stable snapshot.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/dashboard.test.ts > uses repeatable-read isolation so concurrent writes cannot tear dashboard metrics

bugs_found.description: getNeedsAttention emitted stale workspace hrefs (`/red-flags`, `/communications?status=failed`, `/program?tab=invites`) that no longer match the live event routes, so clicking dashboard attention cards led to dead ends.
bugs_found.fix: Updated attention hrefs to the current event workspace routes: `/flags`, `/communications/failed`, and `/faculty/invite`.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/dashboard.test.ts > includes correct hrefs scoped to eventId

attacks_attempted.payload: call createEvent as org:ops or org:read_only via direct server-action invocation
two tabs read draft event, one cancels while the other publishes using the stale copy
invalid moduleToggles JSON, invalid event UUIDs, empty/oversized slugs
read/update another event by guessed UUID through getEvent and updateEventStatus
oversized event name/slug/module toggle payloads within and above schema limits
SQL metacharacters in slug/name/module toggle fields
load draft event via getEventBySlug

bugs_found.description: createEvent only checked for an authenticated user, so org:ops and org:read_only users could bypass the UI and create new events directly.
bugs_found.fix: Enforced server-side create RBAC so only super admins and event coordinators can create events when Clerk role data is present.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/event.test.ts > createEvent should reject ops users that bypass the UI and call the action directly

bugs_found.description: updateEventStatus performed a fetch-then-update by bare event id. A stale tab could overwrite a concurrent cancellation/archive and the post-write reread would still report success because it observed its own write.
bugs_found.fix: Replaced the blind update with a compare-and-swap update keyed by id, current status, and updatedAt so stale lifecycle transitions fail instead of clobbering newer state.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/event.test.ts > updateEventStatus should fail when the event status changed after it was read

attacks_attempted.payload: call getFailedNotifications/getNotificationDetail/retryNotification/manualResend as org:ops
use org:ops access to read failed notification recipients, bodies, and provider errors from communications logs
request notificationLogId from another event under the active eventId scope
whitespace-only and oversized templateKey filters on getFailedNotifications
101+ character templateKey filter sent directly to the failed-log query path
SQL metacharacters inside templateKey and notificationLogId

bugs_found.description: The notifications action trusted generic event access, so org:ops users could bypass the UI and read failed communication payloads or trigger retry/resend operations even though the communications module is coordinator/admin only.
bugs_found.fix: Added communications-specific read/write role allowlists inside the action: reads now allow super_admin, event_coordinator, and read_only; retries/resends allow only super_admin and event_coordinator.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/notifications.adversarial.test.ts > blocks ops users from reading communications failure logs; blocks ops users from retrying failed notifications

bugs_found.description: getFailedNotifications accepted whitespace-only and arbitrarily long templateKey filters, so direct callers could force pointless oversized filter payloads through validation into auth/database work.
bugs_found.fix: Trimmed templateKey and enforced a 1..100 character limit before auth or query execution.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/notifications.adversarial.test.ts > rejects oversized templateKey filters before auth or database work

attacks_attempted.payload: call create/search/update/archive/anonymize people actions with an org:ops session instead of coordinator/admin
call getEventPeople for another event id while authenticated for a different event
call ensureEventPerson directly to forge an event_people link without proving event write access
non-UUID eventId/personId and arbitrary source strings on event roster/link helpers
submit updatePerson against an archived or anonymized row after the record lifecycle changed in another tab
submit a 501-row importPeopleBatch payload to amplify per-row duplicate checks/inserts
SQL metacharacters in search and create/update text fields
<script>alert(1)</script> in person profile fields

bugs_found.description: The people master actions trusted any authenticated user, so org:ops callers could bypass the hidden/disabled UI and create, search, update, archive, restore, or anonymize people records directly.
bugs_found.fix: Added people-module read/write role allowlists inside the action layer. Reads now require super_admin, event_coordinator, or read_only; writes require super_admin or event_coordinator.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/person.test.ts > blocks ops users from creating people directly; blocks ops users from reading the global people search

bugs_found.description: getEventPeople accepted arbitrary event IDs without validating the UUID or proving event membership, and ensureEventPerson allowed direct callers to forge event_people links without event-scoped write access.
bugs_found.fix: Validated event/person IDs and source values, required assertEventAccess for event roster/link helpers, and blocked ops from reading event rosters by enforcing people-specific event read roles.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/person.test.ts > rejects malformed event ids before event-roster auth or queries; rejects forged event-person links without event write access; blocks ops users from reading event rosters even with event access

bugs_found.description: updatePerson wrote by bare person id, so a stale edit form could repopulate archived or anonymized records after another action had already removed them from active use, undermining the irreversible anonymization path.
bugs_found.fix: Scoped updates to active rows only by requiring archivedAt and anonymizedAt to still be null before write-back.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/person.test.ts > refuses to edit archived or anonymized people

bugs_found.description: importPeopleBatch accepted an unbounded array, letting direct callers multiply duplicate checks and inserts into a single oversized server-action request.
bugs_found.fix: Added a 500-row hard cap before any per-row auth, duplicate lookup, or insert work begins.
bugs_found.regression_test: src/lib/actions/person.test.ts > rejects oversized import batches before doing per-row work
